title: Rubus adenochlamys
intro: Rubus adenochlamys is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to Australia, commonly known as a native raspberry or rock raspberry. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as inflammation, pain, and respiratory conditions due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Rubus adenochlamys include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its health benefits. In herbal medicine, Rubus adenochlamys is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or jam, making it a popular ingredient in traditional remedies for respiratory and skin issues.
